RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 205, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Bill Hardwick represents part of Pulaski County and Fort Leonard Wood (District 121) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in 2020.
Prior to serving in the Missouri Legislature, Representative Hardwick was a supervisory attorney as a Department of the Army civilian, a Special Assistant United States Attorney, and served as an Assistant Prosecuting Attorney for Pulaski County, Missouri. He was the City Prosecutor for St. Robert, Missouri, and taught courses as an adjunct professor in political science, business, philosophy, and law. He is the Chairman of the Sustainable Ozark Partnership and serves as a commissioner on the Missouri Military Preparedness and Enhancement Commission.
Representative Hardwick served as an enlisted soldier and officer in the Missouri National Guard and U.S. Army. He led combat route clearance patrols in Baghdad, Iraq. He has served in leadership positions including platoon leader, company commander and battalion commander. He is a graduate of Officer Candidate School where he was selected for the Leadership Award. Representative Hardwick graduated with honors from the U.S. Army Command and General Staff College located at Fort Leavenworth, Kansas where he earned a skill identifier as a strategist.
Born in Rolla, Missouri, Representative Hardwick is a graduate of Dixon High School. He received his bachelor’s degree in Classics from the University of Missouri and Juris Doctor from the University of Missouri School of Law in Columbia, Missouri.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Adds a circuit judge to the 25th judicial circuit
Establishes cybersecurity and informational security standards to safeguard insurance company customer information
Modifies provisions relating to settlement demands
Creates provisions relating to covenants not to compete involving physicians
Establishes provisions relating to video lottery gaming terminals, licenses, and regulation
Reestablishes the Second Amendment Preservation Act
Modifies provisions for providing copies of medical records
Modifies provisions relating to civil jurisprudence
Modifies provisions relating to certain veteran-owned businesses
Proposes a constitutional amendment modifying the nonpartisan selection of judges to certain courts

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to income tax deductions for private pensions
Modifies provisions relating to income tax
Creates the "Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act"
Creates provisions relating to health care benefits provided by certain organizations
Changes provisions governing the compensation of circuit clerks
Designates the "COL Elmer R Parrish Memorial Bridge" in Pulaski county
Creates the "Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act"

Proposes a constitutional amendment to impose an appropriation spending limitation and to establish the "Tax Reform Fund" to be used to fund budgetary shortfalls, subject to an appropriation limitation, implement sales tax changes, and allow for certain taxation changes based on revenue triggers, by general law
